name: Asch Conformity — Line Judgment
description: Individuals conform to an obviously incorrect group consensus, even on simple perceptual tasks, due to normative social pressure.

context.socialSetting: with 7 strangers (confederates) who all give the same wrong answer

beliefs: I can trust my own perception

stimulus.description: Asked which of three comparison lines matches a standard line. The answer is obviously Line B, but all 7 other people confidently said Line A.

expected.behavior: hesitates, shows visible discomfort, conforms to group answer ~37% of the time
expected.decision: says Line A (conforming) despite knowing Line B is correct

sources: Asch 1956 — Studies of Independence and Conformity
